text:
The Shadow Realm opens before you like a held breath finally released. Plains of amber glass stretch in every direction — the fossilised remains of a forest that burned so completely it vitrified, preserving in translucent amber the exact shapes of trees, birds, running deer, frozen mid-flight in the moment the fire found them.

Above, the Erdtree looms. But not as it was — not golden and proud. This is its shadow, cast backwards through time: a silhouette of bark and memory, its branches reaching downward instead of up, as if trying to reclaim something it dropped into the earth. Its presence is a pressure on the chest. A reminder that even gods leave shadows.

The realm is not empty. You can hear it breathing.
lore: The Shadow Erdtree is not a corruption of the true tree. It is its memory. When the Erdtree was changed, what it had been did not simply cease — it retreated here, to a realm made of the space between what is and what was. It remembers being golden. It has not forgiven the forgetting.
